rationale: The citing paper references Dinner, Van Heerde, and Neslin (2014) once in the introduction as a single exception to the claim that offline-to-online effects are underexplored. It is used as background positioning in a short literature contrast, not as a source for hypotheses, constructs, modeling choices, or interpretation of results. The core framework of the citing study is agglomeration effects on eWOM volume, and the cited paper does not play a direct functional role in that framework.
